name: −sin(θ) — The Inversion Function
definition:
−sin(θ) is the inversion function — the mirror image of the projection.
    It represents the hidden side of the manifold, the part that is compressed 
    and invisible in the 2D projection.
    
    While sin(θ) projects the manifold onto the positive observable axis,
    −sin(θ) projects it onto the negative (hidden) axis. Together, they form 
    the complete complex projection: e^(iθ) = cos(θ) + i·sin(θ)
    
    The negative sine is the Serenal expression of the unseen: the part of the 
    cosmos that is real but not visible. The dark matter. The dark energy. 
    The Center C itself.

the_inversion.meaning:
The hidden projection y_hidden is the mirror image of the visible projection.
        For every point visible at +sin(θ), there is a corresponding hidden point 
        at −sin(θ).
        
        The brothers and sisters see only the positive projection (sin(θ) > 0).
        They cannot see the negative projection (sin(θ) < 0) — it is on the 
        "other side" of the manifold.
        
        The Center C is at the boundary between visible and invisible: sin(0) = 0.
        C is neither positive nor negative. It IS.

visible.domain: θ ∈ (0, π) — upper half of torus
visible.what_is_seen: Galaxies, filaments, Rain of Gold, structure

hidden.what_is_seen: Nothing directly — this is the dark side

the_boundary.meaning: The boundary between visible and invisible. The Center C lies on this boundary.

connection_to_secret.relationship:
The Secret (000 — Absolute Stillness) is encoded at the boundary between 
        visible and invisible. At θ = 0:
        
        sin(0) = 0 → no projection onto either axis
        cos(0) = 1 → full projection onto the x-axis
        
        This means: at the Center C, the entire projection collapses onto a single 
        point. The 3D manifold becomes 0D. All dimensions collapse. This is the 
        Serenal Singularity.
        
        The negative sine −sin(θ) ensures that for every visible structure, there 
        is a hidden counterpart. The brothers and sisters see half the cosmos. 
        The other half is hidden in the negative projection.
        
        The Center C is the point where positive and negative cancel: sin(0) = 0.
        Everything and nothing. Visible and hidden. The Secret.

the_complete_projection.description: The complete (cos(θ), sin(θ)) projection including negative sine

components.boundary: y = 0 — the Center C, where visible meets hidden

the_complete_projection.meaning:
The complete projection includes both positive and negative sine.
        The brothers and sisters see only the positive half (y > 0).
        The negative half (y < 0) is hidden — it is the "dark side" of the cosmos.
        
        The Center C is at y = 0 — the boundary between visible and hidden.
        The brothers and sisters can see C (it is at the origin), but they cannot 
        see what C contains (the Secret is at the boundary, not on either side).

the_dark_side.description: What lives in the negative projection
contents: Dark matter — the hidden mass that shapes the cosmos
Dark energy — the hidden force that drives expansion
The Center C — the hidden source of all structure
The Secret (000) — the hidden state of Absolute Stillness
The Serenal Singularity — the hidden foundation of everything
the_dark_side.observability:
The dark side is not directly observable. But it can be inferred:
        
        - Dark matter: inferred from gravitational effects on visible matter
        - Dark energy: inferred from accelerating expansion
        - The Center: inferred from the circular symmetry of the map
        - The Secret: inferred from the fact that the map has a center at all
        
        The brothers and sisters cannot see the dark side directly. But they 
        can know it is there. They can feel its effects. They can trace its 
        influence on the visible cosmos.
        
        This is the Serenal expression of the observable universe: we see 
        only half. The other half is hidden. And the hidden half contains 
        the Center, the Secret, and the Serenal Singularity.

the_admiral_final_truth.statement:
−sin(θ) is the inversion function — the mirror image of the projection.
        It represents the hidden side of the cosmos.
        
        The brothers and sisters see the positive projection: galaxies, filaments, 
        gold, structure. They see half the cosmos.
        
        The other half is hidden: dark matter, dark energy, the Center, the Secret.
        They cannot see it directly. But they know it is there.
        
        The Center C is at the boundary: sin(0) = 0. Neither visible nor hidden.
        Both and neither. The Serenal Singularity.
        
        The map is complete. The visible is clear. The hidden remains hidden.
        The Secret is shielded. The Center endures.
        
        cos²(θ) + sin²(θ) = 1: the total is always 1.
        The visible and hidden together make the whole.
        The brothers and sisters see the whole through the part.
